dublinCore.dc:title: 111 S4890 IS: Protecting Indian Water Rights Settlements Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Luján (for himself and Mr. Heinrich) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Indian Affairs

form.official-title: To amend the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act to establish subaccounts in the Indian Water Rights Settlement Completion Fund, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Indian Water Rights Settlements Act of 2026.

section.header: Indian Water Rights Settlement Completion Fund subaccounts

subsection.text: Section 70101 of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (25 U.S.C. 149) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Operations, Maintenance, Repair, and Ongoing Obligations Subaccount

paragraph.text: There is established in the Fund a subaccount, to be known as the Operations, Maintenance, Repair, and Ongoing Obligations Subaccount (referred to in this subsection as the Subaccount).

subparagraph.text: On the later of October 1, 2026, and the date of enactment of this subsection, and on each October 1 thereafter through October 1, 2035, out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the Secretary of the Treasury shall deposit in the Subaccount $45,000,000.

Amounts deposited in the Subaccount under subparagraph (A) shall be available to the Secretary of the Interior, without further appropriation or fiscal year limitation, for the uses described in paragraph (3).

paragraph.text: Amounts deposited in the Subaccount under paragraph (2)(A) shall be used by the Secretary of the Interior, without further appropriation or fiscal year limitation, for transfers to funds or accounts authorized to receive discretionary appropriations, including Tribal trust funds established by an Act of Congress, to satisfy the obligations of the United States with respect to certain Indian water rights settlements under the following:

subparagraph.text: The water delivery and operation, maintenance, repair, and related provisions of Public Law 98–530 (98 Stat. 2698), relating to the water rights of the Ak-Chin Indian Community.

The water delivery and operations, maintenance, repair, and related provisions of the Colorado Ute Indian Water Rights Settlement Act of 1988 (Public Law 100–585; 102 Stat. 2973), relating to the Animas-La Plata Project (as defined in section 3 of that Act (102 Stat. 2973)).
Section 10603(g) of the Northwestern New Mexico Rural Water Projects Act (Public Law 111–11; 123 Stat. 1387).

The water acquisition provisions of the Snake River Water Rights Act of 2004 (Public Law 108–447; 118 Stat. 3431), including to carry out the Snake River Flow Component described in section 5(a) of that Act (118 Stat. 3433).

The Hualapai Tribe Water Rights Settlement Act of 2022 (Public Law 117–349; 136 Stat. 6225), including the Hualapai Water Trust Fund Account established under section 6(a)(1) of that Act (136 Stat. 6234).

paragraph.text: There is established in the Fund a subaccount, to be known as the New and Continuing Settlements Subaccount (referred to in this subsection as the Subaccount).

subparagraph.text: On the later of October 1, 2026, and the date of enactment of this subsection, and on each October 1 thereafter through October 1, 2035, out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the Secretary of the Treasury shall deposit in the Subaccount $250,000,000.

Amounts deposited in the Subaccount under subparagraph (A) shall be available to the Secretary of the Interior, without further appropriation or fiscal year limitation, for the uses described in paragraph (3).

paragraph.text: Subject to paragraph (4), amounts deposited in the Subaccount under paragraph (2)(A) shall be used by the Secretary of the Interior for transfers to funds or accounts authorized to receive discretionary appropriations, including Tribal trust funds established by an Act of Congress, or to satisfy other obligations of the United States identified by the Secretary of the Interior, under any Indian water rights settlement approved and authorized by an Act of Congress.

subparagraph.text: Transfers authorized under paragraph (3) shall be made in such amounts as are determined by the Secretary of the Interior to be appropriate to satisfy the obligations of the United States, including appropriate indexing, pursuant to the applicable Indian water rights settlement.

The Secretary of the Interior shall have the discretion to determine the sequence and timing of transfers from the Subaccount under paragraph (3) to substantially complete the eligible Indian water rights settlements as expeditiously as practicable.

subsection.text: Section 312(e) of the White Mountain Apache Tribe Water Rights Quantification Act of 2010 (Public Law 111–291; 124 Stat. 3095; 136 Stat. 6184) is amended by striking paragraph (5) and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any amounts made available under paragraph (2)(B) shall not be made available from the Reclamation Water Settlements Fund established by section 10501(a) of the Omnibus Public Land Management Act of 2009 (43 U.S.C. 407(a)).
